title: Integrity and ethical values
implementation_statement: Northwind maintains a code of conduct acknowledged by all employees at hire and annually. Violations are handled by HR and Security.

title: Security organization and competence
implementation_statement: A dedicated Security and GRC organization reports to the CTO; roles and responsibilities are documented.

title: Internal communication of policies
implementation_statement: Security policies are published internally and reviewed at least annually; employees are notified of changes.

title: Risk assessment
implementation_statement: Management performs a formal risk assessment at least annually covering security, vendor, and operational risks; risks are documented and tracked to remediation.

title: Control monitoring
implementation_statement: Northwind monitors the operation of controls through continuous monitoring tooling and internal audits.

title: Technology general controls
implementation_statement: Logical access, change management, and operations controls are implemented across production systems.

title: Logical access provisioning
implementation_statement: Access to production is role-based, approved, and granted on least-privilege. Workforce identity uses Okta SSO with mandatory MFA.

title: Registration and authorization
implementation_statement: New access requires manager and system-owner approval through a ticketed workflow before provisioning.

title: Access reviews
implementation_statement: Privileged and production access is reviewed quarterly; identified discrepancies are remediated.
notes: Q3 2025 review for one team completed late — see SOC 2 Section 4 exception CC6.3.

title: Encryption in transit
implementation_statement: All external and internal service traffic is encrypted with TLS 1.2 or higher.

title: Encryption at rest
implementation_statement: Customer data is encrypted at rest using AES-256 via Cloud KMS (GCP) and AWS KMS.

title: Customer-managed encryption keys
implementation_statement: CMEK / bring-your-own-key is not yet supported. Planned for H2 2026.
notes: Honest negative — answer No on questionnaires until shipped.

title: Vulnerability detection
implementation_statement: Continuous vulnerability scanning runs across containers, dependencies, and infrastructure; findings are triaged by severity SLA.

title: Security monitoring
implementation_statement: Security events are centrally logged and monitored 24/7 with alerting to on-call security.

title: Incident evaluation
implementation_statement: Identified security events are evaluated against the incident response plan to determine severity and response.

title: Incident response
implementation_statement: A documented incident response plan defines roles, severity levels, communications, and customer notification. The plan is tested at least annually.

title: Change control
implementation_statement: Code and infrastructure changes follow peer review, automated testing, and approval before deployment via CI/CD.

title: Vendor risk management
implementation_statement: Third parties are risk-assessed at onboarding and reviewed periodically; DPAs are required for processors handling customer data.

title: Capacity and backup
implementation_statement: Capacity is monitored; backups are performed and tested. Multi-region failover supports availability commitments.

title: Recovery testing
implementation_statement: Disaster recovery is tested at least annually against documented RTO (4h) and RPO (1h) for core services.

title: Confidential data identification
implementation_statement: Confidential data is classified and handled per the data classification policy; customer content is treated as confidential.

title: Confidential data disposal
implementation_statement: Confidential data is securely deleted at end of retention or upon customer request within 30 days.

title: Consent and choice
implementation_statement: Privacy program aligns to ISO 27701; enterprise/API customer content is not used to train base models by default (opt-in only).

title: Data subject requests
implementation_statement: Processes exist to support data subject and customer deletion/access requests within contractual timeframes.

title: Background checks
implementation_statement: Background checks are performed on employees where permitted by law prior to start.

title: Security awareness training
implementation_statement: All employees complete security awareness training at hire and annually; phishing simulations are run periodically.

title: Secure SDLC
implementation_statement: Security is integrated into the SDLC including code review, SAST/DAST, and dependency scanning.

title: Penetration testing
implementation_statement: An independent penetration test is performed at least annually; findings are tracked to remediation by severity.
notes: One High finding from the 2026 test remains in remediation — see pentest summary.

title: Business continuity plan
implementation_statement: A business continuity plan is documented and reviewed annually covering critical functions.

title: Bug bounty
implementation_statement: A public bug bounty program is not currently operated. A private vulnerability disclosure process exists; public bounty is planned.

title: FedRAMP authorization status
implementation_statement: Northwind AI is not FedRAMP authorized and does not currently hold a FedRAMP Authorization to Operate (ATO); FedRAMP is out of scope for the current product offering and no authorization is in progress. Customers with US federal requirements should contact us about the roadmap.
notes: Compliance-posture control documenting the FedRAMP position so the honest "No, not FedRAMP authorized" answer is grounded and citeable (a customer-facing statement of certification scope).
